name: Four-Interaction Mass-Effect Response
rejected: false transport-to-mass shortcut introduced by misunderstanding C-309
scalar potential curvature as canonical mechanism
mass_effect_tensor: M_ij = d^2 E4 / (dv_i dv_j) at v=0
separation: Mass Effect is local velocity response; 125 GeV is finite work to the Mirror threshold.
blocker: derive W_i and one independent microscopic energy calibration
failure_conditions: fit response matrix from measured masses
discrete_shift: delta_v Z_i = -Delta t * v_j * D_j Z_0,i + O(v^2)
discrete_mass_tensor: M_jk = sum_i DeltaV (D_j Z_0,i)^T W_i (D_k Z_0,i)
work_metric: W_i is a four-interaction, positive-semidefinite work metric with diagonal and cross-interaction blocks; it must be derived from the update rules.
force_separation: F_i = M_ij*a_j + C_ij*v_j + ...; C*v is drag/attenuation, not Mass Effect.
frame_constraint: Any constant-velocity drag relative to Ground must pass C-313 preferred-frame audit.
canonical_guard: Never derive Mass Effect from propagation speed, damping, localization share, or any local/transport ratio. Use the complete four-interaction response tensor only.
claim_gate_detail: GREEN (One-Wave mechanism identity) / YELLOW (profiles, response coefficients, and numerical spectrum)
